# GAIA

> GAIA is the Claude Code workflow for React teams: a project-level layer of rules, hooks, agents, memory, and CI that gives Claude enough structure to be trusted as the sole engineer on the project, turning raw capability into work you can ship. Engineering leaders at growing teams use GAIA to make AI trustworthy enough to own features end-to-end. The AI reads GAIA's enforced coding rules, runs its code-review-audit agent on every pull request, follows Agentic Design patterns purpose-built for React, and writes to a shared Obsidian wiki so project knowledge survives across sessions. A standing CI layer keeps dependencies current, patches security findings, syncs the wiki, and prunes stale branches automatically. The result: a production-ready React codebase built at AI speed, with the structure and discipline that holds under pressure. Claude is raw power. GAIA is order and focus.

GAIA installs with one command, `npx create-gaia@latest my-app`, and scaffolds a React 19 project with the workflow built in: enforced coding rules, a code-review-audit agent on every merge, Agentic Design patterns, an Obsidian wiki for durable project knowledge, a full test stack (Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright, MSW, Storybook, Chromatic), and a standing CI layer that keeps dependencies current, patches security findings, syncs the wiki, and prunes stale branches. Open source, written by one developer (so far). Contributors and sponsors welcome.

## Site

- [GAIA: The Claude Code Workflow for React](https://gaiareact.com/): What GAIA is, who it's for, and the core idea: structure that holds under pressure.
- [Why Claude Code Breaks Without Structure](https://gaiareact.com/why/): The case for project-level discipline in AI-built code, and why vibe coding falls apart at scale.
- [Features](https://gaiareact.com/features/): Coding rules, code-review audits, Agentic Design patterns, the Obsidian wiki, token economics, and GAIA CI, covered pattern by pattern.
- [Get Started](https://gaiareact.com/get-started/): Install GAIA in one command, and what `npx create-gaia` sets up.
- [Consulting](https://gaiareact.com/consulting/): Hands-on help adopting GAIA and Claude Code: Audit, Migration, Foundation, or Retainer, by the creator.
- [About Steven Sacks](https://gaiareact.com/about/): Creator of GAIA React, and the original GAIA Flash Framework back in 2008.

## Docs

- [GAIA Documentation](https://docs.gaiareact.com/): Full reference for GAIA's features, commands, hooks, agents, and CI workflows.

## Code

- [GAIA on GitHub](https://github.com/gaia-react/gaia): Source of truth for features, rules, hooks, skills, and commands. MIT-licensed.

## npm

- [create-gaia on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-gaia): Scaffold a new GAIA project: `npx create-gaia@latest my-app`
